dev.jeka.core.api.depmanagement

Class JkCoordinateDependency

  • java.lang.Object
    • dev.jeka.core.api.depmanagement.JkCoordinateDependency
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    JkDependency, JkTransitivityDependency

    public final class JkCoordinateDependency
extends java.lang.Object
implements JkTransitivityDependency
    A dependency on an external module supposed to be located in a binary repository.

    The version or version range identify which versions are likely to be compatible with the project to build.
    For example, both org.hibernate:hibernate-core:3.0.+ and org.hibernate:hibernate-core:3.0.1 are a legal descriptions for module dependency.

    You can also define exclusions on module dependencies so artifact or entire module won't be catch up by the dependency manager.

      • getIdeProjectDir

        public java.nio.file.Path getIdeProjectDir()
        Description copied from interface: JkDependency
        In the IDE, a dependency can be provided by a project exporting it. When generating IDE metadata files (.classpath, xxx.iml), it's preferable to mention only the project exporting it rather than dependency itself. This method simply returns null when the dependency is not coming from another project.
        Specified by:
        getIdeProjectDir in interface JkDependency

      • withIdeProjectDir

        public JkCoordinateDependency withIdeProjectDir(java.nio.file.Path path)
        Description copied from interface: JkDependency
        Returns a dependency identical to this one but with the specified project base dir.
        Specified by:
        withIdeProjectDir in interface JkDependency
        See Also:
        JkDependency.getIdeProjectDir()

      • withVersion

        public JkCoordinateDependency withVersion(JkVersion version)

      • matches

        public boolean matches(JkDependency other)
        Description copied from interface: JkDependency
        Returns true if the specified dependency matches with this one.

        Matching means that two matching dependencies can not be declared in a same dependency set as it will be considered as a duplicate or result in a conflict.

        For example "com.google:guava:21.0" is matching with "com.google:guava:23.0" even if they are not equals.

        Specified by:
        matches in interface JkDependency
